QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_8

answer.text: Has only clearing rights and does not have trading rights
Has both trading and clearing rights
Can only clear trades for custodial participants
Is the same as a Trading-cum-Self-Clearing Member

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Professional Clearing Members have only clearing rights and no trading rights. They clear and settle trades executed by trading members and custodian participants. SEBI-registered custodians and banks recognized by clearing corporations are eligible to become PCMs. **The Trap:** PCMs clear for both trading members AND custodial participants, not just custodial participants (option C is incomplete). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V covers the role of PCMs in the clearing and settlement framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PCM = clearing specialist. Eligible entities: SEBI custodians + recognized banks. They earn by providing clearing services to trading members who don't have their own clearing membership.
